_status: TODO: tradurre in de — testo inglese usato come segnaposto
p00_narration.text: Eurbania is no longer the city you remembered. The Community Center is still here: more alive than an abandoned building, more fragile than a truly safe place. The rooms are open, the notice boards full, the chairs mismatched. But something is off. Some posters are faded. Some doors stay closed. On the digital map, the districts at the margins seem to lose colour.
hub.text: The Community Center hall is full of voices. Who do you want to talk to?
hub.choice.6: Join Anna at the digital map
anna_1.text: Look at this place. Still standing. Still noisy. Still impossible to keep tidy.
anna_2.text: It is. But alive doesn't mean healthy. We saved the Community Center, yes. But the city around it changed faster than we did.
anna_3.text: Some people come here every week. Others don't even know this place is also for them. Some can't reach it. Some don't trust it. Some are too tired to take part.
anna_4.text: Now we stop asking the city to come to the center. We bring the center into the city.
ben_1.text: A meeting is only public if people can actually get there.
ben_2.text: Ramps and doors, of course. But also buses, schedules, language, fear, children, money, shame. Accessibility has many stairs, my friend.
ben_3.text: Exactly. And they're often the ones nobody measures.
sys_ben.text: Map note added: “Accessibility is not only physical.”
giorgia_1.text: They keep saying “the community is tired.” As if a community were one person who needs a nap.
giorgia_2.text: No. Some are tired because they carry everyone's weight. Others because nobody ever asks them to carry anything that really matters.
giorgia_3.text: Rest, time, care. Everything becomes political when it's missing.
sigrid_1.text: See those chairs? Broken. That table? Wobbly. That shelf? Don't breathe near it.
sigrid_2.text: Sounds normal. Things break. The question is: do people know they're allowed to fix them?
sigrid_3.text: Then a shared space becomes just a room full of nobody's objects.
ruben_1.text: I've been reading the online comments.
ruben_2.text: Democracy is unhealthy. Fascinating, but unhealthy. Listen to this: “The Community Center is just a club for people who already know each other.”
ruben_3.text: That's the problem with ugly sentences. Sometimes they're not completely false.
ruben_4.text: Only if it becomes more interesting than the usual good intention.
amin_1.text: At the bar, people say things they would never say in an assembly.
amin_2.text: Because nobody starts with a PowerPoint.
amin_2.choice.0: So the bar is more democratic than the Town Hall?
amin_3.text: I didn't say that. But at the bar you can at least tell when someone isn't listening to you.
sys_amin.text: Map note added: informal spaces matter.
map_1.text: Come. Let me show you something.
map_3.text: This is Eurbania now. You know the centre. But the city has expanded, or maybe we just stopped ignoring some of its parts.
map_8.text: The map glitches. Connection lines break for one second.
map_9.text: Maybe. Bugs are easier to fix than cities.
res_1.text: I don't want to reduce people to numbers. But we have to observe what usually stays invisible.
res_3.text: They get small when you have to find a key, a chair, a person to open the door.
res_4.text: And they get complicated when someone asks who pays.
res_5.text: Yes. Town Hall. And before anyone says it: no, I didn't bring unlimited funds.
alex_2.text: The Town Hall is opening a citywide consultation on neighbourhood resilience. We need the Community Center involved. But this time, we need more than good intentions. We need people, evidence, priorities and a plan.
alex_4.text: With those who never make it into the room.
alex_5.text: And with what we can realistically change.
alex_6.text: Wonderful. Reality, obstacles and expectations. My favourite triad.
alex_7.text: Main Quest unlocked: Same City, Different Routes.
end_prologue.text: End of the prologue. Next chapter: five people, the same city, different routes to the same assembly. (Demo — next development cycle)
ui.title: Justurbanities — Demo
char_select.text: Same city, different routes. Eurbania is about to call an assembly — who will you be?
char_select.choice.2: Elena — civic worker, badge and doubts
char_select.choice.3: Luca — father, plus one small citizen
char_select.choice.4: Someone new — write your own route
route_router.text: The day of the consultation arrives. Across Eurbania, different mornings lead to the same evening.
maya_r1.text: Maya has read the announcement four times. Each time, the same thought: this is for people who already know how to talk.
maya_r2.text: An assembly. With microphones, probably. And adults who say “governance” without laughing.
maya_r3.text: I could just… not go. Nobody would notice. Except me, every day, for a while.
maya_r3.choice.0: Go, with one written sentence in your pocket.
maya_r3.choice.1: Go, but promise yourself to only listen.
maya_r4.text: The bus is late. She gets on anyway. The sentence in her pocket weighs less at every stop.
samir_r1.text: The assembly starts at six. Samir's shift ends at six. The city, as usual, assumes everyone's time bends the same way.
samir_r2.text: I can swap with Dario. Again. Three favours this month — I'm becoming a debt with legs.
samir_r3.text: Or I could skip it. Decisions get made anyway. Usually about people like me, rarely with people like me.
samir_r3.choice.0: Swap the shift. Some rooms you have to be in.
samir_r4.text: He arrives with the helmet still under his arm. Nobody minds. Someone hands him a chair.
elena_r1.text: Elena prepared the folder twice: once as Town Hall, once as herself. Tonight she would like to leave the badge in a drawer.
elena_r2.text: If I speak first, it's institutional. If I stay silent, it's indifference. Public service is a sport with strange rules.
elena_r3.text: The consultation needs the institutions in the room. It also needs people to forget they're there. Ideally at the same time.
elena_r3.choice.0: Leave the badge. Arrive as a neighbour.
elena_r3.choice.1: Wear the badge. Doors must open tonight.
elena_r4.text: On the way she answers two emails and one doubt. Only the doubt was urgent.
luca_r1.text: Sofia's babysitter cancelled at five. The assembly is at six. Luca knows this equation: it never balances.
luca_r2.text: Bring a six-year-old to a public assembly. What could possibly go wrong.
luca_r3.text: Or I stay home. Again. The city can be decided by people whose evenings are free. It usually is.
luca_r3.choice.0: Bring Sofia. Cities are also decided by people with crayons.
luca_r3.choice.1: Ask Amin to watch her at the pub for an hour.
luca_r4.text: He arrives — slightly late, slightly proud, with one more citizen than expected.
custom_r1.text: You don't have a story in this city yet. That is exactly why tonight matters: assemblies are where strangers practise becoming neighbours.
custom_r2.text: First time? Good. We need eyes that still notice things.
custom_r3.text: There's room in the front row, and there are chairs to set up in the back. Both count.
custom_r3.choice.1: Help set up the chairs first.
custom_r4.text: The room fills slowly, like trust.
c2_end.text: Everyone is here — or almost. The assembly is about to begin. (Demo — the assembly itself arrives with the next development cycle.)
